name: Stardew Valley
description: You've inherited your grandfather's old farm plot in Stardew Valley. Armed with hand-me-down tools and a few coins, you set out to begin your new life. Can you learn to live off the land and turn these overgrown fields into a thriving home?
requirements:
Minimum:
  • OS *: Windows Vista or greater
  • Processor: 2 Ghz
  • Memory: 2 GB RAM
  • Graphics: 256 mb video memory, shader model 3.0+
  • DirectX: Version 10
  • Storage: 500 MB available space
comment1:
Spent the first 2 years trying to do everything and never having enough time. 

Days upon days of constantly running around trying to find the town folk who asked for help and celebrating every festival/birthday only to pass out at 2am and have my pockets molested.

Things are way different in the DPRK.
Thanks to the Glorious Leaders Immaculate Initiative to stop crime we don't deal with things like this.

I knew I beat the game when I gifted enough alcohol and cake to give the whole town liver failure and diabetes so the Racoon family could invade.
comment2:
Stardew Valley was one of those games that I had heard about for years and simply had no interest in. It isn't my style, it isn't the type of content I play at all. But one day I was so unbelievably bored that I thought, f*ck it- let's play Stardew Valley. My friends all love it so I might as well.

 I logged 14 hours the first time I opened the game.

The thing is, I could play this game on repeat forever and doesn't even grace my top 3 games. 

It's fun, addictive, wholesome, somewhat dark and even frustrating. The townspeople feel so genuine to be around. I love getting to know them all over again in every run on the game. I'm aiming for 1 save per marriage candidate. 
I love designing my farm, the feeling of euphoria when you reach Ginger Island for the first time... your first batch of starfruit jelly....
I won't consider this a con on the game, but for some reason this is the ONLY game on my pc that crashes. Nothing else, save for maybe a sims crash every fortnight. Anyway. 

Really fun. Going to get a tattoo of a fairy rose when I can.

The only actual cons I can think of is a lack of new diaglouge and events once you finish year 2. Maybe need some new townies or something. Fix Shane's alcoholism after you marry him or something
comment3: my dog's passing really allowed me to start enjoying the relaxing parts of life. cheers to more Stardew Valley.
